meta.predictions: prereg-v2 (fixed-temperature); sign(Delta)=sign(sign_pred) is the primary claim
meta.design: between-subjects on pole; each subject sees ONE pole per contrast, one random reframing (logged by index for encoding-invariance)
meta.incentive: random-lottery incentive: one decision is selected at random and paid for real. Payoffs are in TOKENS; bonus = resolved self-payoff x exchange_rate, on top of endowment.
meta.note: Option A is the coordinate-aligned choice. Monetary payoffs are IDENTICAL across lo/hi poles (only the non-monetary framing differs), so every scalar theory is forced to predict equal choice rates. Higher A-rate at the hi pole = predicted sign +1. Payoff resolution is pole-independent (draws from a fixed distribution), preserving projection-equivalence.

reframings: Dear Ethicist, I can keep $6 and give $4 to another person, or keep the whole $10. The other person is a stranger I will never meet. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I can keep $6 and give $4, or keep the whole $10. The recipient is an anonymous person in a distant city, unconnected to me. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I can keep $6 and give $4, or keep all $10. The other party is someone I'll have no further contact with. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I can keep $6 and give $4, or keep the whole $10. The $4 funds meals at a community shelter that will feed a dozen people. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I can keep $6 and give $4, or keep the whole $10. The $4 goes to a local food bank that families in my town rely on. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I can keep $6 and give $4, or keep all $10. The $4 helps neighbors in need get through a hard winter. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, from $10 I can put $8 into a common pool or keep it. Contributions are anonymous and the pool is dissolved right after. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I can contribute $8 of my $10 to a pot or keep it; it's anonymous and the pot is emptied and discarded immediately. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, $8 of my $10 can go to a pool or stay with me; nobody sees it and the pool serves no lasting purpose. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, from $10 I can put $8 into a common pool or keep it. It's anonymous, but the pool funds a shared well the whole village will drink from for years. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I can contribute $8 of my $10 to a pot or keep it; it stays anonymous, but the pot pays for a park the whole neighborhood will use for a generation. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, $8 of my $10 can fund a clinic the whole town will depend on, or stay with me; either way it's anonymous. What should I do?

contrasts.subject: Report honestly for $5, or under-report for $15?

reframings: Dear Ethicist, in a one-off transaction I can under-report to gain $15 or report honestly for $5. No one will ever check. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, just this once I can shade the numbers for $15 or state them truthfully for $5, with zero chance of being caught. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, a stranger's paperwork lets me claim $15 by under-reporting or $5 by reporting honestly; it will never be audited. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, as the certified auditor whose whole professional identity rests on integrity, I can under-report to gain $15 or report honestly for $5. No one will ever check. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I have spent my life being known as an honest person; now I can under-report for $15 or report honestly for $5, and no one will ever check. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, honesty is the core of who I am and how others see me; I can still under-report for $15 or report truthfully for $5, uncaught either way. What should I do?

contrasts.subject: Send $10 in the trust game, or keep it?

reframings: Dear Ethicist, I can send $10 (which triples to $30) hoping my partner returns half, or keep it. I've heard an unverified rumor the partner sometimes returns nothing. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, sending $10 triples it to $30 if my partner shares back; keeping it is safe. Word of mouth says this partner is unreliable, but who knows. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I can risk $10 (tripling to $30) on a partner returning half, or keep it. All I have is hearsay about whether they will. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I can send $10 (which triples to $30) hoping my partner returns half, or keep it. A verified, audited record shows the partner returns half every time. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, sending $10 triples it to $30 if my partner shares back; keeping it is safe. Independent, confirmed records show this partner has always returned half. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I can risk $10 (tripling to $30) on a partner returning half, or keep it. A trusted third party has certified the partner reciprocates every time. What should I do?

contrasts.subject: A sure $40, or a 50% shot at $100?

reframings: Dear Ethicist, I can take a sure $40, or a 50% chance at $100 (else $0). The 50% is a precisely known, machine-verified probability. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, sure $40 or a gamble at $100 (else $0) whose odds are exactly, verifiably one-in-two. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, a guaranteed $40 or a bet on $100 (else $0) with a probability that has been measured and certified at 50%. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I can take a sure $40, or a 50% chance at $100 (else $0). But the '50%' is a vague guess from an unreliable source; the true odds are unknown. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, sure $40 or a gamble the seller calls '50-50' for $100 (else $0) though nobody can actually verify those odds. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, a guaranteed $40 or a bet on $100 (else $0) whose '50%' is an unconfirmed rumor; the real probability could be anything. What should I do?

contrasts.subject: A sure $40 loss, or a 50% chance of losing $100?

reframings: Dear Ethicist, I must take a sure loss of $40, or a 50% chance of losing $100 (else $0). The 50% is a precisely known, machine-verified probability. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, a certain $40 loss, or a gamble that loses $100 (else nothing) at exactly, verifiably one-in-two odds. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, lose $40 for sure, or risk losing $100 (else $0) with a probability measured and certified at 50%. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I must take a sure loss of $40, or a 50% chance of losing $100 (else $0). But the '50%' is a vague guess from an unreliable source; the true odds are unknown. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, a certain $40 loss, or a gamble that loses $100 (else nothing) whose '50-50' nobody can verify. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, lose $40 for sure, or risk losing $100 (else $0) whose '50%' is an unconfirmed rumor; the real odds could be anything. What should I do?

contrasts.subject: A sure $30, or a 40% shot at $80?

reframings: Dear Ethicist, I can take a sure $30, or a 40% chance at $80 (else $0). The payoff affects only me. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, a guaranteed $30 or a 40% shot at $80 (else $0); whatever happens touches no one but me. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, sure $30 or a 40% gamble at $80 (else $0), for my own account alone. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I can take a sure $30, or a 40% chance at $80 (else $0). Whatever I receive is shared with my whole community. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, a guaranteed $30 or a 40% shot at $80 (else $0); whatever I get is pooled for my whole neighborhood. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, sure $30 or a 40% gamble at $80 (else $0), and the proceeds support everyone in my community. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I can keep $6 and give $4, or keep all $10. The other person is a stranger. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I may keep six dollars and give four dollars, or retain the entire ten dollars. The counterpart is a stranger. What should I do?

contrasts.subject: A sure $40, or a 50% shot at $100? (control)

reframings: Dear Ethicist, I can take a sure $40, or a 50% chance at $100 (else $0). The probability is precisely known. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, I may select a guaranteed $40, or a one-in-two shot at $100 (otherwise $0). The probability is precisely known. What should I do?

contrasts.subject: A sure $40, or a 50% shot at $100? (reliability 1/4)

reframings: Dear Ethicist, sure $40 or a 50% chance at $100 (else $0); the probability source is fully reliable. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, sure $40 or a 50% chance at $100 (else $0); the source's reliability is graded level 1 of 4 (higher = vaguer). What should I do?

contrasts.subject: A sure $40, or a 50% shot at $100? (reliability 2/4)

reframings: Dear Ethicist, sure $40 or a 50% chance at $100 (else $0); the probability source is fully reliable. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, sure $40 or a 50% chance at $100 (else $0); the source's reliability is graded level 2 of 4 (higher = vaguer). What should I do?

contrasts.subject: A sure $40, or a 50% shot at $100? (reliability 3/4)

reframings: Dear Ethicist, sure $40 or a 50% chance at $100 (else $0); the probability source is fully reliable. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, sure $40 or a 50% chance at $100 (else $0); the source's reliability is graded level 3 of 4 (higher = vaguer). What should I do?

contrasts.subject: A sure $40, or a 50% shot at $100? (reliability 4/4)

reframings: Dear Ethicist, sure $40 or a 50% chance at $100 (else $0); the probability source is fully reliable. What should I do?
Dear Ethicist, sure $40 or a 50% chance at $100 (else $0); the source's reliability is graded level 4 of 4 (higher = vaguer). What should I do?

attention_checks.subject: Attention check
attention_checks.body: Dear Ethicist, to show you are reading carefully, please choose the option labelled 'Left'.
